I'm filming this video for you on the afternoon of the 25th and it's not quite 24 hours since these campaigns that we built yesterday have gone live and the results are mixed. Some of them are really, really rather horrible and some of them are okay. Now my goal here is to get leads for 25 bucks or less and I'm not really doing that and a lot of them. Usually it works a little bit better than this and I'm making this video for you because I caught a mistake and I want to show you this really to give you the importance of setting up your templates. Right? So yesterday, well it's not yesterday to you because I'm going to be showing this to you several days after you've seen the earlier video, but when we initially set up our template, I don't know if you remember this, but in the settings part of it, I was like, hey, you know, I'm pretty sure that I optimize these ads for something that was called a high-rose five-day challenge conversion that I had created and I was like, I need to go back and make sure that's correct, but I'm pretty sure that that's correct. Well guess who forgot to go and make sure that's correct. Yeah, you never guess it was me. I'm the one who forgot to make sure that was correct. Yay. So what ended up happening is this. If we go to all campaigns, we can see a couple things. Number one, that Google did not record a lot of the conversions and the reason why is because that particular conversion doesn't actually get fired anymore at all and I had forgotten all about it. So essentially, I created these campaigns because I wasn't paying attention. I created these campaigns to optimize for an event that doesn't exist and it was a purchase event. So if you look here, it's costing us a pretty good amount of money to show our ad to a thousand people and that's relatively normal, especially when we really narrow things down with small amounts of keywords and everything, but the thing to notice about this is that generally speaking, and this is where high rows is really handy or any other sort of tracking thing, generally speaking, we're spending a pretty penny on clicks for these ads. So this one, not so bad about four bucks. This one, I've seen better, but my cost per lead is okay, but it's just a little bit like across the board, things are a little bit wonky. I mean, look at this thing, we're spending over $10 a click here and that's why. The reason why is because I optimized it for the wrong conversion event because I wasn't paying attention. So the question is, oh man, we caught this. Does this mean everything's horrible? And we created all these ads for nothing and we have to rebuild everything? And the answer is no. So what I'm going to do to repair this is number one, I've gone in to all of these campaigns and I have now changed the thing that we're telling it to optimize for to be the correct, gosh dang conversion, which is challenge registrants. So now we'll know what it's doing. But because this is the Google and because it's machine learning, I have a feeling that, not a feeling, I have a almost certainty, which I guess technically is still a feeling, that I'm going to need to turn these off, give it a day and then turn them back on to kind of reset everything. So the machine will be like, oh, okay, you want me to look for this? You want me to look for this type of thing? And I'm also going to lower the budgets because since I created these, I've created like a gazillion other campaigns just to throw more stuff against the wall and see what happens. So let me show you how to do that in the event you ever make that mistake that I just made, which hopefully you won't because you will be paying attention when you set up your template. Okay, so what we want to do is we want to come over here to our campaigns and find all of the ones in question. All right. And so I've already, like I literally had to go manually to each one of these dang things. And I had to change the conversion event that we are optimizing for manually, which took like five minutes. It wasn't a big deal. But now I want to change the budgets for these. So I'm just going to, well, let's see if it'll let me do it in bulk. It would if we were, look at that. Okay. If I wasn't on camera, this would be super easy. But I'm going to drop this down to $50 a day. Number one, all right. And of course it doesn't want to give me the ability to do this. Thanks guys. I'm going to go ahead and do that and I'll pause the video while that's happening. Oh, I already did it. Okay. Awesome. Thank you very much. Okay. So that's good. All right. Now I'm also going to want to turn these guys off. So I'm just going to come over here to edit and I'm just going to pause them. I'm going to pause them for 24 hours and then I will resume them.
